message:
test(readme): cover DataFrameIoExt to_* + SqlInspector (fd90.290 / br-67lca)

Extends readme_quick_start_round_trip_through_sqlite to actually call
6 trait/struct items previously in prelude but never exercised:

DataFrameIoExt trait methods:
- by_ticker.to_parquet_bytes() — Vec<u8>
- by_ticker.to_feather_bytes() — Vec<u8>
- by_ticker.to_excel_bytes() — Vec<u8>
- by_ticker.to_sql(&conn, "via_trait", SqlIfExists::Replace)
- by_ticker.to_sql_with_options(&conn, "via_trait_opts", &write_opts)

SqlInspector (schema introspection per README line 148):
- SqlInspector::new(&conn).tables(None) — list tables in default schema

API observation: SqlInspector method is `tables(schema)`, not
`list_tables` as initial guess.

Verified: 36 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -167,6 +167,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenpandas-60du","title":"[HIGH] docs: CHANGELOG.md 671 commits / 12 days behind HEAD; stats table understates state by ~2-9x per row","description":"CHANGELOG.md maintenance audit.

**Observation.** `CHANGELOG.md` is 536 lines / ~48 KB. Last modified by `git log -1 -- CHANGELOG.md`: **2026-04-10 23:50:20** — "Harden IO column validation".

**Count of commits since last CHANGELOG update:** `git log --oneline <last-changelog-sha>..HEAD | wc -l` → **671 commits**.

**Stats table at the end of CHANGELOG.md (lines 527-536) is also wildly stale:**
  Total commits | 269                       ← reality: 269 + 671 = ~940
  Date range    | 2026-02-13 to 2026-03-17  ← reality extends to 2026-04-22 (today)
  Tags / releases | None (pre-release)       ← still true, but closer to 0.1.0
  Workspace crates | 12                     ← still correct
  Lines of Rust | 84,000+                    ← reality likely 120k+ given recent epics
  Public functions | 788                    ← reality: 1530 (verified 2026-04-22 via grep in br-7cfm)
  Conformance packets | 55+                  ← reality: 430 (verified 2026-04-22 in br-n609)

**Consequence.**
- CHANGELOG.md is the canonical summary a user hitting docs.rs / crates.io / GitHub Releases reads to understand what's in the project. It's 12 days + 671 commits behind reality. Every new user lands on an outdated picture.
- br-4clx (release workflow) will tag v0.1.0 with CHANGELOG.md as release-notes input. If we tag today, the notes say "55+ conformance packets" while reality is 430 — ~9x understatement.
- The CHANGELOG format (features / fixes / chore grouped by conventional-commit prefix) could be auto-generated by release-plz or git-cliff, but is currently hand-maintained. Nobody will keep up with 671-commits-every-12-days cadence by hand; the swarm's velocity outpaces manual documentation.
- br-4clx proposes release-plz for the publish workflow. release-plz IS the CHANGELOG automation — same tool. Landing 4clx closes this gap as a side-effect, but 4clx is itself blocked on br-1d9y (nightly pin), br-36qc (cargo-audit), br-0l5r (cross-platform CI). Meanwhile CHANGELOG keeps drifting.

**Severity.** HIGH — user-facing credibility at release. Also: a CHANGELOG that's stale for 12 days + 671 commits on an active swarm is effectively abandoned documentation, regardless of whether a release ships.

**Fix shape:**

1. Short-term (ship today or tomorrow): one catch-up commit. Use `git log --pretty='format:- %s'` between the last CHANGELOG sha and HEAD, filter by conventional-commit prefix (feat / fix / test / chore / docs), append to CHANGELOG under an "Unreleased" section. Regenerate the stats table using:
   - `git log --oneline | wc -l` (total commits)
   - `grep -c "^pub fn" crates/*/src/lib.rs` (public fns — use the script from br-7cfm once it ships, but a one-shot grep works for now)
   - `ls crates/fp-conformance/fixtures/packets/*.json | wc -l` for packet count
   - `find crates -name '*.rs' | xargs wc -l` for LOC

2. Medium-term: adopt `git-cliff` (or release-plz) with a `cliff.toml` that maps conventional-commit prefixes to CHANGELOG sections. Run in CI as a "chore(changelog)" auto-commit on merge to main. Preserves the Unreleased section until `git-cliff --bump --tag v0.1.0` rolls it forward.

3. Link CHANGELOG entries to bead IDs: the project already uses `(br-frankenpandas-XXXX)` suffix on most commit subjects; `git-cliff` can extract those and link to `.beads/issues.jsonl` for traceability.

4. Once CI auto-commits the CHANGELOG, drop the hand-maintained stats table or auto-regenerate it (same script from fix 1).

Blast radius: 1 one-shot catch-up commit (~200-400 lines appended to CHANGELOG.md), +1 cliff.toml (~30 lines), +1 ci.yml job (~15 lines), +1 line in CONTRIBUTING.md (br-6d5s) pointing at the new auto-regeneration flow. Zero source-logic changes.

Prerequisites:
- None urgent for the one-shot catch-up.
- br-4clx (release workflow) for the automated flow — they should ship together.

**Related:**
- br-4clx (release workflow + release-plz) — this bead becomes moot after 4clx lands (release-plz handles changelogs natively). File separately so it can be closed quickly with a one-shot catch-up.
- br-6d5s (CONTRIBUTING.md) — the changelog policy lives here.
- br-n609 (COVERAGE.md auto-generator) + br-zk1j (API matrix) — sibling auto-generated-doc beads. All three replace hand-maintained stats.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:16:29.279408814Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:05:29.471477679Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:05:29.469013654Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["changelog","docs","release","review"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-63ac","title":"fd90.51: SqlReflectedTable accessor methods for columns / indexes / UCs / FKs","description":"Add accessor methods on SqlReflectedTable mirroring the SqlTableSchema::column shape: column(name) -> Option<&SqlColumnSchema>, index(name) -> Option<&SqlIndexSchema>, unique_constraint(name) -> Option<&SqlUniqueConstraintSchema>, foreign_keys_for_column(col) -> Vec<&SqlForeignKeySchema>. Pure ergonomic accessors over the named-Vec fields. No new behavior. Tests verify each accessor on a real SQLite reflection bundle and on missing names.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-26T03:40:40.380451501Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T03:43:51.235786656Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T03:43:51.233287125Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","fd90","review","sql"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-641fd","title":"fd90.152: FEATURE_PARITY.md packet evidence shows ~21 of 430 packets — add note pointing to actual ledger","description":"fd90.74-151 audit. FEATURE_PARITY.md (referenced from README's Key Documents at line 1666 as 'Detailed parity matrix with status per feature family') has a 'Phase-2C Packet Evidence' table at lines 51-75 listing only ~21 packets:
  - 11 FP-P2C packets (001-011)
  - 10 FP-P2D packets (025, 026, 027, 028, 029, 030, 031, 032, 039, 040)

But the actual fp-conformance fixture coverage is much larger:
  ls crates/fp-conformance/fixtures/packets/ | grep -oE 'fp_p2[cd]_[0-9]+' | sort -u | wc -l
  → 430 distinct packet suites
  - 11 FP-P2C (matches README exactly)
  - 419 FP-P2D (FEATURE_PARITY.md shows 10 of 419 = 2.4% coverage)

The table grew dramatically since FEATURE_PARITY.md was last refreshed; it's now misleading users who read it expecting comprehensive parity status.

Two ways to fix:
A. Auto-regenerate the table from artifacts/phase2c/ — out of scope for a 60-min slice (would need a script).
B. Add a clarifying note: 'Below is the foundational subset; the full live packet ledger is in artifacts/phase2c/drift_history.jsonl with 430 total packet suites.'

Going with B for this slice — the README's Conformance section already gives the actual count. Pure docs-only fix to align expectations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T14:36:46.273393093Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T14:38:03.259855379Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T14:38:03.257173150Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-67lca","title":"fd90.290: Cover DataFrameIoExt to_* methods + SqlInspector","description":"fd90.290: Cover DataFrameIoExt trait methods + SqlInspector.

The DataFrameIoExt trait (in prelude) is documented at README line 528 with 11 to_* methods:
- to_parquet, to_parquet_bytes
- to_csv_file, to_json_file, to_jsonl_file
- to_excel_file, to_excel_bytes
- to_feather_file, to_feather_bytes
- to_sql, to_sql_with_options

None of these are called from any test. Plus SqlInspector (in prelude per fd90.206) is also never called.

Scope:
- Extend an existing test with df.to_parquet_bytes / to_feather_bytes / to_excel_bytes calls + SqlInspector usage. Skip path-form tests (require tmp dir).

Files: crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs.
Time estimate: 15-20min.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-27T11:12:11.845979675Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T11:14:48.014178076Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T11:14:48.011971797Z","close_reason":"Extended readme_quick_start_round_trip_through_sqlite to cover 6 trait/struct items previously in prelude but never called: DataFrameIoExt.to_parquet_bytes / to_feather_bytes / to_excel_bytes (3 bytes-form trait methods), DataFrameIoExt.to_sql / to_sql_with_options (2 SQL trait methods), SqlInspector::new(&conn).tables(None) (schema introspection — README docs 'tables / views / schemas / columns / indexes / FKs / UCs / reflect_table'). API observation: SqlInspector method is , not  as I initially typed. All 36 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-6919","title":"fp-columnar Column.rolling_window_sum sliding reducer","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:00:48.479381441Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:03:15.735721617Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T08:03:15.733365798Z","close_reason":"Implemented Column::rolling_window_sum in commit fe8b05e, 5 tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-6crfb","title":"fd90.250: Integration test for RuntimePolicy + EvidenceLedger Bayesian flow","description":"fd90.250: Add integration test for RuntimePolicy + EvidenceLedger Bayesian decision flow.

The README's Bayesian Runtime Policy section (lines 378-403) describes:
- RuntimePolicy::strict() / hardened()
- RuntimePolicy.decide_unknown_feature / decide_join_admission
- EvidenceLedger.records() returning &[DecisionRecord]
- DecisionAction::{Allow, Reject, Repair}

None of this is integration-tested. fd90.221 added the inspection types to the prelude; this bead exercises an actual decision flow + inspection.

Scope:
- Add a new test (or extend an existing one) that:
  - Constructs RuntimePolicy::strict() and ::hardened(None)
  - Calls decide_unknown_feature or decide_join_admission
  - Inspects the returned DecisionAction
  - Reads EvidenceLedger.records() and pattern-matches the record fields

Files: crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs.
Time estimate: 20-30min.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-27T06:34:28.400754081Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T06:36:52.525363280Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T06:36:52.523195394Z","close_reason":"Added readme_bayesian_runtime_policy_compiles_and_runs covering the Bayesian decision flow documented at README lines 378-403: RuntimePolicy::strict() / ::hardened(None), policy.decide_unknown_feature (asserted DecisionAction::Reject under strict), policy.decide_join_admission(1_000_000, &mut ledger), EvidenceLedger.records() inspection (asserted RuntimeMode::Strict, DecisionAction::Reject, IssueKind::UnknownFeature, non-empty evidence Vec), decision_to_card(record). API observation: decide_join_admission takes (estimated_rows, &mut ledger) — only 2 args, not 3 as I initially expected. All 36 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
